phases.title: Security & Identity
phases.description: Establish who users are, what they can do, and how secrets are kept safe. These are the non-negotiable foundations before any real traffic touches your app.

phases.title: Money & Data Integrity
phases.description: Handle payments correctly, model subscriptions robustly, and ensure your data never lies. Mistakes here are business-critical and often irreversible.

phases.title: Observability
phases.description: Instrument your application so you can understand its behavior in production. You cannot improve what you cannot measure.

phases.title: Reliability & Scale
phases.description: Design your system to stay up, recover from failure, and handle growth without rewriting everything.

phases.title: Delivery & Operations
phases.description: Ship safely and repeatedly. Automate the path from code to production and give yourself levers to control rollouts.

phases.title: Incident Management
phases.description: Prepare for things going wrong: detect, respond, recover, and learn. The difference between a blip and a disaster is process.

phases.title: Growth & Governance
phases.description: Operate sustainably at scale: understand your customers, control costs, maintain compliance, and document your systems for the team.

phases.title: AI-Assisted Development
phases.description: Get useful, correct, safe output from AI coding tools — prompting, context, agents, evals, security, cost. Vendor-neutral patterns for working with Claude, Cursor, Copilot, ChatGPT, and beyond.

phases.title: Modern Frontend
phases.description: Browser-side concerns the production-readiness phases assume but never teach: bundling, rendering strategies, hydration, type-safe APIs, web vitals, design systems.

phases.title: Modern Backend & Platform
phases.description: Backend topics adjacent to production-readiness: monorepos, dev containers, type-safe ORMs, edge runtimes, realtime protocols, durable jobs, vector search.

phases.title: Developer Experience & Craft
phases.description: The human side: local environment, debugging mindset, code review craft, git workflows, documentation-driven development, sustainable pace.

authentication.title: Authentication
authentication.summary: Verify user identity securely using industry-standard protocols such as OAuth 2.0, OIDC, and session management best practices.

access-control.title: Access Control
access-control.summary: Enforce who can do what in your application using RBAC, ABAC, or policy-based authorization systems.

secrets-management.title: Secrets Management
secrets-management.summary: Store, rotate, and audit API keys and credentials using dedicated vaults rather than hardcoding them in source or environment files.

rate-limiting.title: Rate Limiting
rate-limiting.summary: Protect your APIs and infrastructure from abuse, scraping, and denial-of-service attacks by capping request rates per client.

cookies.title: Cookies & Consent
cookies.summary: Manage first- and third-party cookies correctly for security (SameSite, HttpOnly, Secure) and obtain lawful consent for tracking under privacy regulations.

gdpr-ccpa.title: GDPR / CCPA Compliance
gdpr-ccpa.summary: Implement data subject rights (access, deletion, portability) and consent flows to comply with major global privacy regulations.

payments.title: Payments
payments.summary: Integrate payment processors securely to accept one-time and recurring charges while handling card validation, fraud, and PCI scope.

billing-logic.title: Billing Logic
billing-logic.summary: Model invoicing, proration, credits, refunds, and dunning correctly so that money in the database always matches money in the payment processor.

subscription-states.title: Subscription States
subscription-states.summary: Model the full lifecycle of a subscription (trial, active, past-due, paused, canceled) as an explicit state machine with correct feature access at each state.

idempotency.title: Idempotency
idempotency.summary: Design APIs and background jobs so that retrying an operation never produces duplicate side effects such as double-charges or duplicate records.

data-integrity.title: Data Integrity
data-integrity.summary: Use database constraints, transactions, and application-level validation to guarantee that stored data is always valid and consistent.

schema-migrations.title: Schema Migrations
schema-migrations.summary: Apply database schema changes safely in production using expand-contract patterns and zero-downtime migration strategies.

crud-logic.title: CRUD Logic
crud-logic.summary: Designing reliable create, read, update, and delete operations: soft deletes, optimistic locking, bulk-operation safety, and audit trails.

logging.title: Logging
logging.summary: Emit structured, queryable logs from all application components so that the sequence of events leading to any outcome can be reconstructed.

alerting.title: Alerting
alerting.summary: Configure threshold- and anomaly-based alerts on metrics and logs so that on-call engineers are notified of production problems before customers are.

instrumentation.title: Instrumentation
instrumentation.summary: Add metrics, counters, histograms, and spans throughout your code to expose system health and business behavior to your observability stack.

distributed-tracing.title: Distributed Tracing
distributed-tracing.summary: Propagate trace context across service boundaries to visualize end-to-end request flows and pinpoint latency bottlenecks in distributed systems.

slos-and-slis.title: SLOs & SLIs
slos-and-slis.summary: Define measurable reliability targets (SLOs) backed by concrete indicators (SLIs) and error budgets to make data-driven decisions about reliability vs. velocity.

cloud-costs.title: Cloud Cost Management
cloud-costs.summary: Monitor, attribute, and optimize cloud spending using tagging, budgets, rightsizing, and reserved capacity to prevent bill shock at scale.

scalability.title: Scalability
scalability.summary: Design application and data layers to handle order-of-magnitude growth in traffic and data volume without fundamental rearchitecting.

latency-optimization.title: Latency Optimization
latency-optimization.summary: Identify and eliminate the dominant sources of request latency using profiling, query analysis, and architectural changes like caching and async work.

caching.title: Caching
caching.summary: Apply in-process, distributed, and CDN caching at the right layer with correct invalidation strategies to reduce latency and database load.

load-balancing.title: Load Balancing
load-balancing.summary: Distribute traffic across multiple application instances to eliminate single points of failure and enable horizontal scaling.

message-queues.title: Message Queues & Job Processing
message-queues.summary: Offload slow or failure-prone work to asynchronous job queues to improve response times, decouple services, and absorb traffic spikes.

dead-letter-queues.title: Dead-Letter Queues
dead-letter-queues.summary: Capture messages that repeatedly fail processing into a separate queue for inspection and replay so that poison messages never silently disappear.

multi-region-support.title: Multi-Region Support
multi-region-support.summary: Deploy across geographic regions to reduce latency for global users and maintain availability during regional cloud provider outages.

error-handling.title: Error Handling
error-handling.summary: Principled strategies for catching, surfacing, containing, and recovering from errors in production systems, including graceful degradation and user-facing vs. internal error distinctions.

ci-cd.title: CI / CD
ci-cd.summary: Automate the build, test, and deployment pipeline so that every merged change can reach production safely with minimal manual intervention.

environments.title: Environments
environments.summary: Maintain distinct development, staging, and production environments with environment-specific configuration and data isolation.

rollbacks.title: Rollbacks
rollbacks.summary: Design deployments so that a bad release can be quickly reverted without data loss using strategies like blue-green deployments and database migration reversibility.

feature-flags.title: Feature Flags
feature-flags.summary: Decouple feature deployment from feature release using runtime flags that allow gradual rollouts, instant kill-switches, and targeted user targeting.

test-coverage.title: Test Coverage
test-coverage.summary: Build a layered test suite of unit, integration, and end-to-end tests that catches regressions before they reach production.

ab-testing.title: A/B Testing
ab-testing.summary: Run controlled experiments by exposing different product variants to user segments and measuring statistically significant differences in behavior.

vendor-lock-in.title: Vendor Lock-In
vendor-lock-in.summary: Assess and mitigate dependency on specific cloud providers or SaaS vendors through abstraction layers, open standards, and portability planning.

api-versioning.title: API Versioning
api-versioning.summary: Evolve APIs without breaking existing clients: URL/header versioning, deprecation policies, and backward-compatibility contracts.

incident-response.title: Incident Response
incident-response.summary: Execute a repeatable process for detecting, communicating, mitigating, and resolving production incidents with defined roles and runbooks.

on-call-rotations.title: On-Call Rotations
on-call-rotations.summary: Establish sustainable on-call schedules, escalation policies, and toil-reduction practices so that production issues always reach a human quickly without burning out the team.

disaster-recovery.title: Disaster Recovery
disaster-recovery.summary: Define and regularly test RPO/RTO targets, backup procedures, and recovery playbooks to restore service after catastrophic failures.

data-retention.title: Data Retention
data-retention.summary: Define how long different classes of data are stored, archived, and deleted to balance compliance obligations, cost, and recovery needs.

escalations.title: Escalations
escalations.summary: Define clear escalation paths for technical incidents and customer-impacting issues so the right people are looped in at the right severity threshold.

retention.title: Retention
retention.summary: Measure and improve how many users return to your product over time using cohort analysis, engagement loops, and lifecycle communications.

churn-control.title: Churn Control
churn-control.summary: Identify at-risk subscribers, trigger proactive interventions, and handle cancellation flows to reduce involuntary and voluntary churn.

support-ops.title: Support Operations
support-ops.summary: Build tooling and processes for customer support, including admin dashboards, audit logs, and runbooks so support staff can resolve issues without direct database access.

governance.title: Governance
governance.summary: Establish engineering standards, change management processes, and audit trails that keep the system compliant and accountable as the organization grows.

documentation.title: Documentation
documentation.summary: Maintain up-to-date runbooks, API references, architectural decision records, and onboarding guides so institutional knowledge survives team changes.

dependency-management.title: Dependency Management
dependency-management.summary: Pin, audit, and update third-party dependencies. Manage supply-chain risk, vulnerability scanning, and license compliance for production software.

prompting-for-code.title: Prompting for Code
prompting-for-code.summary: Ask AI coding tools for code that compiles, runs, and matches your codebase conventions — through clear intent, examples, and constraints.

context-engineering.title: Context Engineering
context-engineering.summary: Curate what an AI tool sees — files, docs, examples, schemas — so it has enough signal to be accurate and not so much that it gets confused.

agent-loops.title: Agent Loops
agent-loops.summary: Autonomous coding agents that plan, edit, run, and verify in a loop. When to use them, how to bound them, what oversight they require.

ai-code-review.title: AI Code Review
ai-code-review.summary: Use AI as a reviewer and as a reviewee — second-opinion workflows, regression checks, and review prompts that catch what tests miss.

mcp-and-tooling.title: MCP and AI Tooling
mcp-and-tooling.summary: Model Context Protocol and the broader pattern of giving AI tools structured access to filesystems, databases, browsers, and your own systems.

ai-evals.title: AI Evals
ai-evals.summary: Measure whether AI features (or AI-assisted workflows) actually work — offline test sets, online metrics, golden examples, regression catches.

hallucination-mitigation.title: Hallucination Mitigation
hallucination-mitigation.summary: Detect and reduce fabricated APIs, made-up types, and false claims from AI output — grounding, verification, and skepticism patterns.

ai-security-and-pii.title: AI Security & PII
ai-security-and-pii.summary: What not to paste into a model, prompt injection, data exfiltration risks, vendor data-handling policies, and how to redact safely.

ai-coding-cost-management.title: AI Coding Cost Management
ai-coding-cost-management.summary: Token economics for AI coding tools — model selection, batching, caching, when the cheapest model is the most expensive because of extra turns.

ai-attribution-and-licensing.title: AI Attribution & Licensing
ai-attribution-and-licensing.summary: Legal and team implications of AI-generated code — copyright, license compatibility, attribution practices, contribution policies.

module-bundlers.title: Module Bundlers
module-bundlers.summary: Vite, Turbopack, esbuild, Rspack — what bundlers do, what they skip, and when native ESM lets you stop bundling for development.

server-rendering.title: Server Rendering Strategies
server-rendering.summary: SSR vs SSG vs ISR vs RSC — trade-offs in latency, hosting cost, freshness, and developer ergonomics for each rendering mode.

hydration-and-islands.title: Hydration and Islands Architecture
hydration-and-islands.summary: Why hydration is slow, how partial hydration and islands (Astro, Qwik, Marko) reduce JS shipped to the browser.

type-safe-api-calls.title: Type-Safe API Calls
type-safe-api-calls.summary: End-to-end types between server and client via tRPC, OpenAPI codegen, or GraphQL codegen — and when to pick which.

state-management-modern.title: Modern State Management
state-management-modern.summary: Zustand, Jotai, Redux Toolkit, signals — and why less client state usually beats a better state library.

image-and-media-pipelines.title: Image and Media Pipelines
image-and-media-pipelines.summary: Modern image formats (AVIF, WebP), responsive images, video streaming basics, and how CDNs handle media transforms.

web-vitals.title: Web Vitals
web-vitals.summary: LCP, INP, CLS — the metrics Google ranks on, how to measure them in the field vs lab, and the highest-leverage fixes.

design-systems.title: Design Systems
design-systems.summary: Tokens, primitives, headless UI, shadcn-style copy-in vs npm-distributed — and when a design system pays for itself.

monorepos.title: Monorepos
monorepos.summary: Turborepo, Nx, pnpm workspaces — when sharing a repo across packages helps, when it hurts, and how to keep CI fast.

dev-containers.title: Dev Containers
dev-containers.summary: Reproducible local environments via devcontainers, nix, or docker compose — the cure for 'works on my machine' bugs.

type-safe-orms.title: Type-Safe ORMs
type-safe-orms.summary: Prisma, Drizzle, Kysely — type-safe queries, migration ergonomics, N+1 prevention, and when to drop the ORM for raw SQL.

edge-runtimes.title: Edge Runtimes
edge-runtimes.summary: Cloudflare Workers, Vercel Edge, Deno Deploy — what runs there, what doesn't, and where the latency wins are real.

realtime-protocols.title: Realtime Protocols
realtime-protocols.summary: Websockets vs SSE vs long-polling — when to choose each, scaling pub/sub, and when NOT to go realtime.

background-jobs.title: Background Jobs
background-jobs.summary: Durable background jobs — BullMQ, pg-boss, Temporal, Inngest — with at-least-once semantics, schedules, and retries.

search-and-vector-store.title: Search and Vector Stores
search-and-vector-store.summary: Full-text search (Postgres FTS, Meilisearch, Typesense) and vector stores (pgvector, Qdrant) — choosing between them and combining.

file-uploads-and-streaming.title: File Uploads and Streaming
file-uploads-and-streaming.summary: Multipart uploads, signed URLs, resumable uploads, virus scanning, and on-the-fly image transforms.

webhooks-as-platform.title: Webhooks as a Platform
webhooks-as-platform.summary: Receiving webhooks safely (idempotency, signing, replay) and emitting them reliably (delivery, retries, observability).

local-dev-environment.title: Local Dev Environment
local-dev-environment.summary: Reproducible setups, editor configuration, language servers, terminal multiplexers — the 5 percent setup investment that compounds.

debugging-mindset.title: Debugging Mindset
debugging-mindset.summary: Systematic debugging: hypothesis-driven investigation, when to add logs vs use a debugger, root cause vs symptom.

code-review-craft.title: Code Review Craft
code-review-craft.summary: The human side of PR review — what good feedback looks like, how to push back kindly, what to nitpick vs let go.

git-workflows.title: Git Workflows
git-workflows.summary: Branching strategies that survive AI tooling, rebase vs merge, conventional commits, and when to squash.

documentation-driven-development.title: Documentation-Driven Development
documentation-driven-development.summary: Write the README first. ADRs as code. Doc tests. Treat documentation as an API contract with future readers.

pair-and-mob-programming.title: Pair and Mob Programming
pair-and-mob-programming.summary: When pairing multiplies output and when it halves it — including the AI pair-programmer as a case in this.

sustainable-pace.title: Sustainable Pace
sustainable-pace.summary: Recognizing burnout, on-call recovery, deep-work cycles — the engineering-discipline version of mental health.
